chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ
اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَكُونُ بِهِ اَلْجُرْحُ فَيَتَدَاوَى بِدَوَاءٍ

text: هِ زَعْفَرَانٌ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ اَلزَّعْفَرَانُ غَالِباً عَلَى اَلدَّوَاءِ فَلاَ وَ إِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْأَدْوِيَةُ غَالِبَةً عَلَيْهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2654 - Imran al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a Muhrim who has a wound and uses a medicine containing saffron.
Imam (as) said: "If the saffron is the dominant ingredient in the medicine, then no. But if the other ingredients are dominant, there is no harm in it."
